name: Duchy of Courland and Semigallia (state flag)
description: A horizontal bicolour of two equal stripes, red over white.
history: The state flag of the Duchy of Courland and Semigallia (1561–1795), a Baltic duchy and vassal of the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, known for its brief colonial ventures under Duke Jacob Kettler. The duchy was annexed by the Russian Empire in 1795.
